body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dependent Arising** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Emptiness from predictive processing — the self as inference, dependent arising as Bayes-optimal perception.
**Phase context:** Predictive processing, causality perception, emptiness, no-self

Paper: Cognitive and behavioural correlates of psychosis in genetic subtypes of Prader-Willi syndrome.
DOI: 10.1016/j.jpsychires.2026.06.054
Abstract: BACKGROUND: Prader-Willi Syndrome (PWS) is a rare neurodevelopmental disorder resulting from the loss of paternally expressed, maternally imprinted genes within the chromosome 15q11-q13 region. While core features such as hyperphagia and developmental delay are nearly universal, the maternal uniparental disomy (mUPD) subtype carries a significantly higher risk of psychosis compared to the paternal deletion subtype. This study investigated the neurocognitive and behavioural mechanisms driving this genotype-specific vulnerability within a predictive processing framework. Prader-Willi Syndrome (PWS) is a rare neurodevelopmental disorder resulting from the loss of paternally expressed, maternally imprinted genes within the chromosome 15q11-q13 region. While core features such as hyperphagia and developmental delay are nearly universal, the maternal uniparental disomy (mUPD) subtype carries a significantly higher risk of psychosis compared to the paternal deletion subtype. This study investigated the neurocognitive and behavioural mechanisms driving this genotype-specific vulnerability within a predictive processing framework. METHODS: Individuals with genetically confirmed PWS (n = 30; 13 mUPD, 16 deletions, 1 unknown case) completed assessments of intellectual ability, executive functioning, and processing speed.
